children.text: Contents: One, The Onset of Contention, page 3. Two, Escalation Dynamics, page 41. Three, Settlement and Aftermath, page 88. Filed under the Halvorne-6 pagination ledger.
children.label: text

children.text: Chapter One: The Onset of Contention
children.label: section_header

children.text: Contentious episodes begin not with the loudest grievance but with the first instance in which previously isolated complaints are recognized by their bearers as instances of a shared condition. This recognition is the true onset, prior to any organized mobilization, and it is the analytic starting point this chapter develops across three illustrative episodes.
children.label: text

children.text: Once a shared grievance is recognized, escalation follows a predictable sequence: informal gatherings widen into semi-formal assemblies, semi-formal assemblies test the state's tolerance for open association, and that test either provokes repression or further widens participation. This chapter traces that sequence across the same three episodes introduced earlier.
children.label: text

children.text: Every episode surveyed here eventually settles, whether through negotiated concession, exhaustion, or suppression, and the settlement's own terms shape whether the underlying grievance resurfaces in a later cycle. This closing chapter compares settlement terms across the three episodes and draws out their shared aftermath dynamics.
children.label: text
